description: Medium: Make XSD section lookup and streaming section-aware at `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Modules/XsdMetadataEndpointModule.cs:149`, `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ContentProvider.cs:96`, and `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ContentProvider.cs:252` so unknown sections do not fall back to core files and streaming opens the same validated manifest path that section-specific file listing exposed.
acceptance-criteria: `/metadata/xsd/{section}/{file}.xsd` returns 404 for an unknown section instead of falling back to core XSD files.
`ContentProvider.Files(fileFullName, ".xsd", section)` does not include core XSD files when the requested section does not match any manifest project.

`XsdMetadataEndpointModule.GetXsdMetadataFileContent` no longer calls `contentProvider.LoadXsdContent(fileFullName)` after only section-scoped validation; it passes the requested section, or an already resolved validated file path, into XSD content loading.
`ContentProvider` resolves the XSD stream with the same project selection rules used by `Files(fileFullName, ".xsd", section)`.
Duplicate XSD file names across extension projects return the requested section's content; core XSD files remain available to extension sections.
Legacy prefixed XSD names normalize consistently for both section-specific listing and streaming.
steps-to-verify: Add a frontend endpoint/unit test that requests an existing core XSD file through an unknown section and assert `/metadata/xsd/{section}/{file}.xsd` returns 404.
Add a frontend unit test with two extension projects that both contain the same XSD file name and assert `/metadata/xsd/{section}/{file}.xsd` returns the requested section's file content.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Xsd"`

description: Medium: Treat a declared `discoverySpecPath` whose file is missing as an invalid ApiSchema workspace instead of silently skipping it and serving another project's discovery spec.
acceptance-criteria: When a manifest project declares `discoverySpecPath`, a missing file causes `LoadJsonContent("discovery", ...)` to throw an `InvalidOperationException` that identifies the invalid project or path.
Only an omitted `discoverySpecPath` is treated as absent optional content; a declared but missing path does not fall back to a later project.
Existing successful discovery-spec placeholder replacement behavior is unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add a `ContentProvider` unit test where the core project declares a missing `discoverySpecPath` and a later extension has an existing discovery spec; assert the provider throws instead of returning the extension spec.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~discovery"`

description: Medium: Treat a declared `xsdDirectory` whose directory is missing as an invalid ApiSchema workspace instead of silently returning no XSD files.
acceptance-criteria: When a manifest project declares `xsdDirectory`, a missing directory causes XSD listing or streaming resolution to throw an `InvalidOperationException` that identifies the invalid project or path.
Only an omitted `xsdDirectory` is treated as absent optional XSD content; a declared but missing directory does not make `/metadata/xsd/{section}` look like a valid empty section.
Existing successful XSD enumeration for present directories remains unchanged, including legacy file-name normalization.
steps-to-verify: Add an `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider` or `ContentProvider` unit test where a project declares a missing `xsdDirectory` and assert XSD enumeration fails with a workspace validation error.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Xsd|FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider"`

description: Low: Reject nested XSD files consistently in `eng/docker-compose/prepare-dms-schema.ps1:454` and `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.cs:325` so staged or bundled XSD content is not silently omitted by runtime top-level enumeration.
acceptance-criteria: `prepare-dms-schema.ps1` fails before writing the staged ApiSchema manifest when a source project's `xsd` or `XSD` directory contains `.xsd` files below a nested subdirectory.

`ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.EnumerateValidatedXsdFiles` detects nested `.xsd` files under a declared `xsdDirectory` and throws `InvalidOperationException` instead of returning only top-level files.
Valid top-level `.xsd` files continue to be listed and streamed with the existing file-name normalization behavior.
The error message identifies the project or nested XSD path that must be flattened.
steps-to-verify: Add Pester coverage for `prepare-dms-schema.ps1` with a project containing `xsd/nested/*.xsd` and assert staging fails before producing a successful manifest.
Add frontend unit coverage for `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.EnumerateValidatedXsdFiles` with a nested `.xsd` under a declared `xsdDirectory` and assert it throws.
Run: `pwsh -NoProfile -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path ./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSchemaDeploymentSafety.Tests.ps1"`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Xsd|FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider"`

description: Medium: Make ApiSchema manifest path validation symlink-aware so schema-adjacent content cannot escape the configured workspace through linked files or directories.
acceptance-criteria: Schema-adjacent content paths are checked against the canonical workspace root before file opens or XSD enumeration.
A symlinked manifest path or `xsdDirectory` that resolves outside the workspace is rejected with `InvalidOperationException`.
Normal in-workspace relative paths still resolve and stream successfully, and symlink tests are skipped on platforms where symlink creation is unavailable.
steps-to-verify: Add unit coverage for an escaping symlink used as `discoverySpecPath` or `xsdDirectory` and assert the provider rejects it before opening or enumerating the target.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider|FullyQualifiedName~ContentProvider"`

description: Medium: Remove runtime `package-manifest.json` synthesis from `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.cs:87` and make bundled ApiSchema content provide the same root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` used by bootstrap workspaces.
acceptance-criteria: `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.GetManifest` reads only the root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` from the configured or bundled ApiSchema workspace and no longer recursively scans for `package-manifest.json` files.
The build or publish path materializes `ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` for bundled ApiSchema package content, with project paths relative to the bundled `ApiSchema` directory.
Running with `UseApiSchemaPath=false` loads bundled schema, discovery spec, and XSD content through the same manifest path validation used when `UseApiSchemaPath=true`.
A stray nested `package-manifest.json` under the ApiSchema workspace is ignored unless it is referenced by the generated root bootstrap manifest.
steps-to-verify: Add or update frontend unit coverage for `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider` showing that a missing root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` fails even when a nested `package-manifest.json` exists.
Add build or publish verification showing `ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` is produced for bundled package content.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider|FullyQualifiedName~ContentProvider"`

description: Medium: Enforce bootstrap workspace-relative path validation in `eng/docker-compose/load-dms-seed-data.ps1:1250` and `eng/docker-compose/load-dms-seed-data.ps1:1332` before reading the staged ApiSchema manifest or copying staged XSDs, so seed loading cannot read XSD content from outside `.bootstrap/ApiSchema`.
acceptance-criteria: `Copy-XsdsFromStagedApiSchema` resolves `schema.apiSchemaManifestPath` through `Resolve-BootstrapWorkspaceRelativePath`, or equivalent validation, before calling `Get-Content`.

Each project `xsdDirectory` is treated as manifest-relative to the staged ApiSchema workspace and rejects rooted paths, empty path segments, current-directory segments, parent-directory segments, and resolved paths outside that workspace before `Test-Path`, `Get-ChildItem`, or `Copy-Item` runs.
Invalid `apiSchemaManifestPath` or `xsdDirectory` values fail with an error that identifies the offending field or project, and no XSD files are copied from an escaping path.
Valid staged `xsdDirectory` values still copy XSD files into the seed workspace `xsd` directory.
steps-to-verify: Add Pester coverage in `eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSeedDelivery.Tests.ps1` for absolute and parent-traversing `xsdDirectory` values in the staged ApiSchema manifest and assert `Copy-XsdsFromStagedApiSchema` throws before copying files.

Add Pester coverage that exercises `Copy-XsdsFromStagedApiSchema` with an invalid `schema.apiSchemaManifestPath` value and assert the helper rejects it through the workspace-relative path validation path.
Run: `pwsh -NoProfile -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path ./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSeedDelivery.Tests.ps1"`

description: Medium: Clear generated ApiSchema package extraction directories before processing `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` so packages removed from the startup configuration do not remain active after a container restart.
acceptance-criteria: The startup package-download path removes stale generated package extraction output under `AppSettings__ApiSchemaPath` before invoking the downloader for the current `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` list.
Cleanup runs only when `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` contains package entries, so bootstrap runs with an empty package list do not attempt to write into the read-only mounted ApiSchema workspace.
Workspace files that are not generated package extraction output, such as `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` and `JsonSchemaForApiSchema.json`, are preserved.
steps-to-verify: Add script-level or integration coverage that starts with stale `ApiSchema/Packages/<RemovedPackage>/package-manifest.json`, processes a package list that omits it, and verifies the removed package is no longer scanned.
Run the focused startup or downloader verification added for this behavior.

description: Medium: Skip default Debezium connector setup for full Docker-stack bootstrap starts, not only DMS-only starts.
acceptance-criteria: `eng/docker-compose/start-local-dms.ps1` skips `setup-connectors.ps1` when bootstrap mode is active in the full-stack startup path.
`eng/docker-compose/start-published-dms.ps1` skips `setup-connectors.ps1` when bootstrap mode is active in the full-stack startup path.
Non-bootstrap full-stack starts still run connector setup unless `-SkipConnectorSetup` is supplied.
The existing DMS-only bootstrap connector skip behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add or update Pester/static coverage that asserts the full-stack connector setup branch in both startup scripts is guarded by bootstrap mode.
Run the focused bootstrap startup script tests for `eng/docker-compose`.

description: High: Include `src/dms/Directory.Build.targets` in the Docker build context at `src/dms/Dockerfile:12` and copy published `ApiSchema/**` content recursively at `src/dms/Dockerfile:66` so bundled ApiSchema package assets and the root bundled manifest declared by `src/dms/Directory.Build.targets:30` survive into the final image.
acceptance-criteria: `src/dms/Dockerfile` copies `Directory.Build.targets` into `/source` before restore/publish so the package-content publish target participates in Docker builds.
The final `src/dms/Dockerfile` image includes the published `ApiSchema/**` content recursively, including `ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json`, discovery/spec files, and XSD files.
Top-level bundled ApiSchema JSON files that are currently copied remain available in the image.
Running without `AppSettings:UseApiSchemaPath=true` loads the bundled root ApiSchema asset manifest instead of depending on runtime package-manifest synthesis.
steps-to-verify: Add a Dockerfile or image-content verification that confirms recursive `ApiSchema` content is copied into the final image.
Build or inspect the DMS image output and verify `/app/ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json`, discovery/spec files, and XSD files are present.
